summary: The implementation is functionally correct but fails to resolve a persistent CodeQL polynomial ReDoS finding (CWE-1333) in the email regex. Three attempts (problem_solver + two security_fixer) all left the same regex unchanged, indicating a fixer loop failure where the security fixer did not modify the vulnerable pattern.
suggested_actions: Replace the regex with a non-backtracking pattern (e.g., use re.fullmatch with a simpler pattern or add length limits to mitigate ReDoS)
Add email length validation before regex matching
Consider using a finite automaton regex or a simpler string check that avoids nested quantifiers

evidence.description: This [regular expression](1) that depends on a [user-provided value](2) may run slow on strings starting with '?@?.' and with many repetitions of '?.'.
evidence.context:
82:

>>  85:         return bool(re.match(r"^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$", email))
